name: Apical Meristem Development
description: Apical Meristem Development in Plants. This process represents a higher-level biological mechanism distinct from molecular/biochemical processes covered in GLMP.
mermaid:
graph TD
    A9[Embryonic Meristem] --> B9[Cell Division Zone]
    B9 --> C9[Cell Elongation Zone]
    C9 --> D9[Cell Differentiation Zone]
    D9 --> E9[Leaf Primordia]
    D9 --> F9[Stem Tissue]
    D9 --> G9[Vascular Tissue]
    E9 --> H9[Organ Formation]
    F9 --> H9
    G9 --> H9
    H9 --> I9[Growth Continuation]
    
    style A9 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style B9 fill:#ffd43b,color:#000
    style C9 fill:#ffd43b,color:#000
    style D9 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style E9 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style F9 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style G9 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style H9 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style I9 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff

sources.title: Programming Framework: A Universal Process Visualization Methodology
sources.notes: Higher-level biological process visualization: Apical Meristem Development. Created using the Programming Framework methodology for organismal and ecological process analysis.

sources.title: The Arabidopsis WUSCHEL gene encodes a novel homeodomain protein that is required for maintenance of shoot meristem stem cells
sources.authors: Schoof H, Busch M, Kulkarni B, Wetterauer S, Kölle K, Käufer F, Schneitz J, Prunelle D, Jürgens G, Simon R.

sources.title: Dependence of stem cell fate on CLV3 signalling and WUSCHEL feedback regulation in the Arabidopsis shoot meristem
sources.authors: Brand U, Hobe M, Simon R.

sources.title: The Shoot Apical Meristem: A Central Control Zone for Plant Development

notes: Higher-level biological process. GLMP covers biochemical/molecular processes; this database covers organismal/ecological processes.
